revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=多久比禮志神社| 所在地=富山県富山市塩690| 祭神=彦火火出見命
豐玉姫命
鹽土老翁| 社格=式内社（小）・郷社| 創建=[[白鳳]]元年（[[672年]]）| 別名=塩宮}} '''多久比禮志神社'''（たくひれしじんじゃ）は、[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧[[上新川郡]][[大沢野町]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。鎮座地名から'''塩宮'''（しおのみや）ともいう。 明治時代に[[式内社]]・多久比禮志神社に比定され現社名に改称したが、式内・多久比禮志神社の論社は他にも数社ある。 ==祭神== [[ホオリ|彦火火出見命]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豐玉姫命]]・[[シオツチノオジ|鹽土老翁]]を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[白鳳]]元年（[[672年]]）4月、林宿禰弥鹿伎が[[神通川]]を船で遡っていると、白髪の老人が現われ、向こうの川辺の松の木の際の泉が塩水であることを伝えると、姿が見えなくなった。一行がその言葉に従って船を進めると、森に囲まれた泉があった。その水を煮つめると[[塩]]が得られた。白髪の老人は[[国魂|国魂神]]で、これはこの地を開拓せよという神託であろうと、社殿を建てて祀ったのが当社の始まりである。鎮座地名の「塩」もこのことに因むものである。 明治時代に式内・多久比禮志神社に比定されたが、「多久比禮」とは栲布（たくぬの）のことであり、当社はむしろ塩に関係のある神社であるので、機織・衣服に関係のある[[姉倉比賣神社]]（富山市呉羽町）や呉服神社（富山市五服）に比定する説もある。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/ecyu/takuhiresi_title.htm 多久比禮志神社]（玄松子） {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} [[Category:富山県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:富山市]]

{{神社 |名称=多久比禮志神社 |画像 = [[File:Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg|250px|社殿]] |所在地=富山県富山市塩690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|scale:10000}} |祭神=彦火火出見命
豐玉姫命
鹽土老翁 |社格=式内社（小）・郷社 |創建=[[672年]]（[[白鳳]]元年） |本殿 = |別名=塩宮 |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''多久比禮志神社'''（たくひれしじんじゃ）は、[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧[[上新川郡]][[大沢野町]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。鎮座地名から'''塩宮'''（しおのみや）ともいう。 明治時代に[[式内社]]・多久比禮志神社に比定され現社名に改称したが、式内・多久比禮志神社の論社は他にも数社ある。 ==祭神== [[ホオリ|彦火火出見命]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豐玉姫命]]・[[シオツチノオジ|鹽土老翁]]を祀る。 ==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[白鳳]]元年（[[672年]]）4月、林宿禰弥鹿伎が[[神通川]]を船で遡っていると、白髪の老人が現われ、向こうの川辺の松の木の際の泉が塩水であることを伝えると、姿が見えなくなった。一行がその言葉に従って船を進めると、森に囲まれた泉があった。その水を煮つめると[[塩]]が得られた。白髪の老人は[[国魂|国魂神]]で、これはこの地を開拓せよという神託であろうと、社殿を建てて祀ったのが当社の始まりである。鎮座地名の「塩」もこのことに因むものである。 明治時代に式内・多久比禮志神社に比定されたが、「多久比禮」とは栲布（たくぬの）のことであり、当社はむしろ塩に関係のある神社であるので、機織・衣服に関係のある[[姉倉比賣神社]]（富山市呉羽町）や呉服神社（富山市五福）に比定する説もある。 {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称=多久比禮志神社 |画像 = [[File:Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg|250px|社殿]] |所在地=[[富山県]][[富山市]]塩690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|scale:10000}} |祭神=彦火火出見命
豐玉姫命

{{nihongo|'''Takuhi Reshi Shrine'''|多久比禮志神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takuhi Reshi Shrine'''|多久比禮志神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takuhi Reshi Shrine'''|多久比禮志神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takuhi Reshi Shrine'''|多久比禮志神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takuhi Reshi Shrine'''|多久比禮志神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takuhi Reshi Shrine'''|多久比禮志神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takuhi Reshi Shrine'''|多久比禮志神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takuhi Reshi Shrine'''|多久比禮志神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takuhi Reshi Shrine'''|多久比禮志神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]].

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=多久比禮志神社 |画像 = [[File:Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[富山県]][[富山市]]塩690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|display=ti}} |祭神=彦火火出見命
豐玉姫命
鹽土老翁 |社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[郷社]] |創建=[[672年]]（[[白鳳]]元年） |本殿 = |別名=塩宮 |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=多久比禮志
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多久比禮志神社'''（たくひれしじんじゃ）は、[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧・[[上新川郡]][[大沢野町]]）塩にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。鎮座地名から'''塩宮'''（しおのみや）ともいう。 明治時代に[[式内社]]・多久比禮志神社に比定され現社名に改称したが、式内・多久比禮志神社の論社は他にも数社ある。 == 祭神 == [[ホオリ|彦火火出見命]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豐玉姫命]]・[[シオツチノオジ|鹽土老翁]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[白鳳]]元年（[[672年]]）4月、林宿禰弥鹿伎が[[神通川]]を船で遡っていると、白髪の老人が現われ、向こうの川辺の松の木の際の泉が塩水であることを伝えると、姿が見えなくなった。一行がその言葉に従って船を進めると、森に囲まれた泉があった。その水を煮つめると[[塩]]が得られた。白髪の老人は[[国魂|国魂神]]で、これはこの地を開拓せよという神託であろうと、社殿を建てて祀ったのが当社の始まりである。鎮座地名の「塩」もこのことに因むものである。 明治時代に式内・多久比禮志神社に比定されたが、「多久比禮」とは栲布（たくぬの）のことであり、当社はむしろ塩に関係のある神社であるので、機織・衣服に関係のある[[姉倉比賣神社]]（富山市呉羽町）や呉服神社（富山市五福）に比定する説もある。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[多久比禮志神社]] to [[Takuhi Reshi Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Merging Japanese Wikipedia import with English content

Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[JA:多久比禮志神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430603}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|多久比禮志神社|version=107328498|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=多久比禮志神社 |画像 = [[File:Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[富山県]][[富山市]]塩690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|display=ti}} |祭神=彦火火出見命
豐玉姫命
鹽土老翁 |社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[郷社]] |創建=[[672年]]（[[白鳳]]元年） |本殿 = |別名=塩宮 |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=多久比禮志
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多久比禮志神社'''（たくひれしじんじゃ）は、[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧・[[上新川郡]][[大沢野町]]）塩にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。鎮座地名から'''塩宮'''（しおのみや）ともいう。 明治時代に[[式内社]]・多久比禮志神社に比定され現社名に改称したが、式内・多久比禮志神社の論社は他にも数社ある。 == 祭神 == [[ホオリ|彦火火出見命]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豐玉姫命]]・[[シオツチノオジ|鹽土老翁]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[白鳳]]元年（[[672年]]）4月、林宿禰弥鹿伎が[[神通川]]を船で遡っていると、白髪の老人が現われ、向こうの川辺の松の木の際の泉が塩水であることを伝えると、姿が見えなくなった。一行がその言葉に従って船を進めると、森に囲まれた泉があった。その水を煮つめると[[塩]]が得られた。白髪の老人は[[国魂|国魂神]]で、これはこの地を開拓せよという神託であろうと、社殿を建てて祀ったのが当社の始まりである。鎮座地名の「塩」もこのことに因むものである。 明治時代に式内・多久比禮志神社に比定されたが、「多久比禮」とは栲布（たくぬの）のことであり、当社はむしろ塩に関係のある神社であるので、機織・衣服に関係のある[[姉倉比賣神社]]（富山市呉羽町）や呉服神社（富山市五福）に比定する説もある。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] ==End Japanese==

Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[JA:多久比禮志神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430603}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|多久比禮志神社|version=107328498|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=多久比禮志神社 |画像 = [[File:Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[富山県]][[富山市]]塩690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|display=ti}} |祭神=彦火火出見命
豐玉姫命
鹽土老翁 |社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[郷社]] |創建=[[672年]]（[[白鳳]]元年） |本殿 = |別名=塩宮 |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=多久比禮志
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多久比禮志神社'''（たくひれしじんじゃ）は、[[富山県]][[富山市]]（旧・[[上新川郡]][[大沢野町]]）塩にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。鎮座地名から'''塩宮'''（しおのみや）ともいう。 明治時代に[[式内社]]・多久比禮志神社に比定され現社名に改称したが、式内・多久比禮志神社の論社は他にも数社ある。 == 祭神 == [[ホオリ|彦火火出見命]]・[[トヨタマヒメ|豐玉姫命]]・[[シオツチノオジ|鹽土老翁]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[白鳳]]元年（[[672年]]）4月、林宿禰弥鹿伎が[[神通川]]を船で遡っていると、白髪の老人が現われ、向こうの川辺の松の木の際の泉が塩水であることを伝えると、姿が見えなくなった。一行がその言葉に従って船を進めると、森に囲まれた泉があった。その水を煮つめると[[塩]]が得られた。白髪の老人は[[国魂|国魂神]]で、これはこの地を開拓せよという神託であろうと、社殿を建てて祀ったのが当社の始まりである。鎮座地名の「塩」もこのことに因むものである。 明治時代に式内・多久比禮志神社に比定されたが、「多久比禮」とは栲布（たくぬの）のことであり、当社はむしろ塩に関係のある神社であるので、機織・衣服に関係のある[[姉倉比賣神社]]（富山市呉羽町）や呉服神社（富山市五福）に比定する説もある。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]]

鹽土老翁 |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|郷社}} |創建={{jalink|672年|Q30601}}（{{jalink|白鳳|Q11580612}}元年） |本殿 = |別名=塩宮 |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=多久比禮志
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多久比禮志神社'''（たくひれしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|富山県|Q132929}}{{jalink|富山市|Q204266}}（旧・{{jalink|上新川郡|Q11359057}}{{jalink|大沢野町|Q8081695}}）塩にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社}}。鎮座地名から'''塩宮'''（しおのみや）ともいう。 明治時代に{{jalink|式内社}}・多久比禮志神社に比定され現社名に改称したが、式内・多久比禮志神社の論社は他にも数社ある。 == 祭神 == {{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|彦火火出見命}}・{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豐玉姫命}}・{{jalink|シオツチノオジ||鹽土老翁}}を祀る。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、{{jalink|白鳳|Q11580612}}元年（{{jalink|672年|Q30601}}）4月、林宿禰弥鹿伎が{{jalink|神通川|Q1156518}}を船で遡っていると、白髪の老人が現われ、向こうの川辺の松の木の際の泉が塩水であることを伝えると、姿が見えなくなった。一行がその言葉に従って船を進めると、森に囲まれた泉があった。その水を煮つめると{{jalink|塩|Q11254}}が得られた。白髪の老人は{{jalink|国魂|Q9692242|国魂神}}で、これはこの地を開拓せよという神託であろうと、社殿を建てて祀ったのが当社の始まりである。鎮座地名の「塩」もこのことに因むものである。 明治時代に式内・多久比禮志神社に比定されたが、「多久比禮」とは栲布（たくぬの）のことであり、当社はむしろ塩に関係のある神社であるので、機織・衣服に関係のある{{jalink|姉倉比賣神社}}（富山市呉羽町）や呉服神社（富山市五福）に比定する説もある。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]]

== {{神社 |名称=多久比禮志神社 |画像 = [[File:Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|富山県|Q132929}}{{jalink|富山市|Q204266}}塩690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|display=ti}} |祭神=彦火火出見命
豐玉姫命
鹽土老翁 |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建={{jalink|672年|Q30601}}（{{jalink|白鳳|Q11580612}}元年） |本殿 = |別名=塩宮 |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=多久比禮志
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多久比禮志神社'''（たくひれしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|富山県|Q132929}}{{jalink|富山市|Q204266}}（旧・{{jalink|上新川郡|Q11359057}}{{jalink|大沢野町|Q8081695}}）塩にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。鎮座地名から'''塩宮'''（しおのみや）ともいう。 明治時代に{{jalink|式内社}}・多久比禮志神社に比定され現社名に改称したが、式内・多久比禮志神社の論社は他にも数社ある。 === 祭神 === {{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|彦火火出見命}}・{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豐玉姫命}}・{{jalink|シオツチノオジ||鹽土老翁}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、{{jalink|白鳳|Q11580612}}元年（{{jalink|672年|Q30601}}）4月、林宿禰弥鹿伎が{{jalink|神通川|Q1156518}}を船で遡っていると、白髪の老人が現われ、向こうの川辺の松の木の際の泉が塩水であることを伝えると、姿が見えなくなった。一行がその言葉に従って船を進めると、森に囲まれた泉があった。その水を煮つめると{{jalink|塩|Q11254}}が得られた。白髪の老人は{{jalink|国魂|Q9692242|国魂神}}で、これはこの地を開拓せよという神託であろうと、社殿を建てて祀ったのが当社の始まりである。鎮座地名の「塩」もこのことに因むものである。 明治時代に式内・多久比禮志神社に比定されたが、「多久比禮」とは栲布（たくぬの）のことであり、当社はむしろ塩に関係のある神社であるので、機織・衣服に関係のある{{jalink|姉倉比賣神社}}（富山市呉羽町）や呉服神社（富山市五福）に比定する説もある。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

鹽土老翁 |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建={{jalink|672年|Q30601}}（{{jalink|白鳳|Q11580612}}元年） |本殿 = |別名=塩宮 |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=多久比禮志
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多久比禮志神社'''（たくひれしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|富山県|Q132929}}{{jalink|富山市|Q204266}}（旧・{{jalink|上新川郡|Q11359057}}{{jalink|大沢野町|Q8081695}}）塩にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。鎮座地名から'''塩宮'''（しおのみや）ともいう。 明治時代に{{jalink|式内社}}・多久比禮志神社に比定され現社名に改称したが、式内・多久比禮志神社の論社は他にも数社ある。 === 祭神 === {{jalink|ホオリ|Q1051960|彦火火出見命}}・{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豐玉姫命}}・{{jalink|シオツチノオジ||鹽土老翁}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 社伝によれば、{{jalink|白鳳|Q11580612}}元年（{{jalink|672年|Q30601}}）4月、林宿禰弥鹿伎が{{jalink|神通川|Q1156518}}を船で遡っていると、白髪の老人が現われ、向こうの川辺の松の木の際の泉が塩水であることを伝えると、姿が見えなくなった。一行がその言葉に従って船を進めると、森に囲まれた泉があった。その水を煮つめると{{jalink|塩|Q11254}}が得られた。白髪の老人は{{jalink|国魂|Q9692242|国魂神}}で、これはこの地を開拓せよという神託であろうと、社殿を建てて祀ったのが当社の始まりである。鎮座地名の「塩」もこのことに因むものである。 明治時代に式内・多久比禮志神社に比定されたが、「多久比禮」とは栲布（たくぬの）のことであり、当社はむしろ塩に関係のある神社であるので、機織・衣服に関係のある{{jalink|姉倉比賣神社}}（富山市呉羽町）や呉服神社（富山市五福）に比定する説もある。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

== {{神社 |名称=Takuhi Reshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{jalink|Toyama Prefecture|Q132929}}{{jalink|Toyama City|Q204266}} Shio 690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|display=ti}} |祭神={{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) / Former {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}} |創建={{jalink|672|Q30601}} ({{jalink|Hakuho|Q11580612}} 1) |本殿 = |別名=Shio no Miya |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takuhi Reshi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takuhi Reshi Shrine''' (たくひれしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Toyama Prefecture|Q132929}} {{jalink|Toyama City|Q204266}} (formerly {{jalink|Kaminiikawa District|Q11359057}} {{jalink|Osawano|Q8081695}}) Shio. Its {{jalink|shrine ranking|Q10444029|former shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}}. It is also called '''Shio no Miya''' (しおのみや) after the name of the place where it is enshrined. During the Meiji era, it was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki}} and was renamed to its current name, but there are several other candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 4th month of the 1st year of {{jalink|Hakuho|Q11580612}} ({{jalink|672|Q30601}}), when Hayashi no Sukune Mikaki was traveling upstream on the {{jalink|Jinzu River|Q1156518}} by boat, a white-haired old man appeared and told him that a spring by the pine trees on the riverbank ahead produced {{jalink|salt|Q11254}} water, and then vanished from sight. Following the old man's words, the party sailed on and found a spring surrounded by a forest. When they boiled down the water, they obtained {{jalink|salt|Q11254}}. The white-haired old man was the {{jalink|Kunitama|Q9692242|Kunitama deity}}, and this was interpreted as a divine oracle to develop this land. A shrine was built to enshrine this deity, and this is the origin of the shrine. The place name "Shio" (salt) also derives from this event. During the Meiji era, the shrine was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine, but "Takuhire" refers to taku cloth (a type of bark cloth), and since this shrine is rather associated with salt, there is also a theory that identifies the Shikinaisha with {{jalink|Anekurahime Shrine}} (in Toyama City Kureha-machi) or Gofuku Shrine (in Toyama City Gofuku), which are associated with weaving and clothing. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{Expand Japanese|多久比禮志神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Takuhi Reshi Shrine | native_name = 多久比禮志神社 | image = Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Takuhi Reshi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.596111|137.19}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Takuhi Reshi Shrine'''|多久比禮志神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|WD=Q135026601}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11636380|lt=Q11636380|WD=Q11636380}} ** Series ordinal: 23 ** P155: {{ill|Q11580592|lt=Q11580592|WD=Q11580592}} ** P156: {{ill|Kumano Shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|WD=Q135040382}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q204266|lt=Q204266|WD=Q204266}} * {{ill|Q906899|lt=Q906899|WD=Q906899}} * {{ill|Nei District, Toyama|lt=Nei district|en|Nei District, Toyama|ja|婦負郡|ko|네이군|zh|婦負郡|WD=Q6987940}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.596111|137.19}} == image (P18) == * Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182408 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|lt=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|lt=Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|WD=Q3029647}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == name in kana (P1814) == * たくひれしじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8230005002663 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '富山県富山市塩690', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:多久比禮志神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430603}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Takuhi Reshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{jalink|Toyama Prefecture|Q132929}}{{jalink|Toyama City|Q204266}} Shio 690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|display=ti}} |祭神={{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) / Former {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}} |創建={{jalink|672|Q30601}} ({{jalink|Hakuho|Q11580612}} 1) |本殿 = |別名=Shio no Miya |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takuhi Reshi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takuhi Reshi Shrine''' (たくひれしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Toyama Prefecture|Q132929}} {{jalink|Toyama City|Q204266}} (formerly {{jalink|Kaminiikawa District|Q11359057}} {{jalink|Osawano|Q8081695}}) Shio. Its {{jalink|shrine ranking|Q10444029|former shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}}. It is also called '''Shio no Miya''' (しおのみや) after the name of the place where it is enshrined. During the Meiji era, it was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki}} and was renamed to its current name, but there are several other candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 4th month of the 1st year of {{jalink|Hakuho|Q11580612}} ({{jalink|672|Q30601}}), when Hayashi no Sukune Mikaki was traveling upstream on the {{jalink|Jinzu River|Q1156518}} by boat, a white-haired old man appeared and told him that a spring by the pine trees on the riverbank ahead produced {{jalink|salt|Q11254}} water, and then vanished from sight. Following the old man's words, the party sailed on and found a spring surrounded by a forest. When they boiled down the water, they obtained {{jalink|salt|Q11254}}. The white-haired old man was the {{jalink|Kunitama|Q9692242|Kunitama deity}}, and this was interpreted as a divine oracle to develop this land. A shrine was built to enshrine this deity, and this is the origin of the shrine. The place name "Shio" (salt) also derives from this event. During the Meiji era, the shrine was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine, but "Takuhire" refers to taku cloth (a type of bark cloth), and since this shrine is rather associated with salt, there is also a theory that identifies the Shikinaisha with {{jalink|Anekurahime Shrine}} (in Toyama City Kureha-machi) or Gofuku Shrine (in Toyama City Gofuku), which are associated with weaving and clothing. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

During the Meiji era, the shrine was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine, but "Takuhire" refers to taku cloth (a type of bark cloth), and since this shrine is rather associated with salt, there is also a theory that identifies the Shikinaisha with {{jalink|Anekurahime Shrine}} (in Toyama City Kureha-machi) or Gofuku Shrine (in Toyama City Gofuku), which are associated with weaving and clothing. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

== {{神社 |名称=Takuhi Reshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{jalink|Toyama Prefecture|Q132929}}{{jalink|Toyama City|Q204266}} Shio 690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|display=ti}} |祭神={{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) / Former {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}} |創建={{jalink|672|Q30601}} ({{jalink|Hakuho|Q11580612}} 1) |本殿 = |別名=Shio no Miya |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takuhi Reshi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takuhi Reshi Shrine''' (たくひれしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Toyama Prefecture|Q132929}} {{jalink|Toyama City|Q204266}} (formerly {{jalink|Kaminiikawa District|Q11359057}} {{jalink|Osawano|Q8081695}}) Shio. Its {{jalink|shrine ranking|Q10444029|former shrine ranking}} was {{jalink|district shrine|Q134917316}}. It is also called '''Shio no Miya''' (しおのみや) after the name of the place where it is enshrined. During the Meiji era, it was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki}} and was renamed to its current name, but there are several other candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 4th month of the 1st year of {{jalink|Hakuho|Q11580612}} ({{jalink|672|Q30601}}), when Hayashi no Sukune Mikaki was traveling upstream on the {{jalink|Jinzu River|Q1156518}} by boat, a white-haired old man appeared and told him that a spring by the pine trees on the riverbank ahead produced {{jalink|salt|Q11254}} water, and then vanished from sight. Following the old man's words, the party sailed on and found a spring surrounded by a forest. When they boiled down the water, they obtained {{jalink|salt|Q11254}}. The white-haired old man was the {{jalink|Kunitama|Q9692242|Kunitama deity}}, and this was interpreted as a divine oracle to develop this land. A shrine was built to enshrine this deity, and this is the origin of the shrine. The place name "Shio" (salt) also derives from this event. During the Meiji era, the shrine was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine, but "Takuhire" refers to taku cloth (a type of bark cloth), and since this shrine is rather associated with salt, there is also a theory that identifies the Shikinaisha with {{jalink|Anekurahime Shrine}} (in Toyama City Kureha-machi) or Gofuku Shrine (in Toyama City Gofuku), which are associated with weaving and clothing. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]]

During the Meiji era, it was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki}} and was renamed to its current name, but there are several other candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines {{jalink|Hoori|Q1051960|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 4th month of the 1st year of {{jalink|Hakuho|Q11580612}} ({{jalink|672|Q30601}}), when Hayashi no Sukune Mikaki was traveling upstream on the {{jalink|Jinzu River|Q1156518}} by boat, a white-haired old man appeared and told him that a spring by the pine trees on the riverbank ahead produced {{jalink|salt|Q11254}} water, and then vanished from sight. Following the old man's words, the party sailed on and found a spring surrounded by a forest. When they boiled down the water, they obtained {{jalink|salt|Q11254}}. The white-haired old man was the {{jalink|Kunitama|Q9692242|Kunitama deity}}, and this was interpreted as a divine oracle to develop this land. A shrine was built to enshrine this deity, and this is the origin of the shrine. The place name "Shio" (salt) also derives from this event. During the Meiji era, the shrine was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine, but "Takuhire" refers to taku cloth (a type of bark cloth), and since this shrine is rather associated with salt, there is also a theory that identifies the Shikinaisha with {{jalink|Anekurahime Shrine}} (in Toyama City Kureha-machi) or Gofuku Shrine (in Toyama City Gofuku), which are associated with weaving and clothing. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}{{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}} Shio 690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|display=ti}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji||WD=|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor) / Former {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建={{ill|lt=672|Q30601|WD=Q30601}} ({{ill|lt=Hakuho|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 1) |本殿 = |別名=Shio no Miya |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takuhi Reshi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takuhi Reshi Shrine''' (たくひれしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}} {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Kaminiikawa District|Q11359057|WD=Q11359057}} {{ill|lt=Osawano|Q8081695|WD=Q8081695}}) Shio. Its {{ill|lt=shrine ranking|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|lt=former shrine ranking}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also called '''Shio no Miya''' (しおのみや) after the name of the place where it is enshrined. During the Meiji era, it was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine listed in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki|1=UNKNOWN}} and was renamed to its current name, but there are several other candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji||WD=|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 4th month of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Hakuho|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} ({{ill|lt=672|Q30601|WD=Q30601}}), when Hayashi no Sukune Mikaki was traveling upstream on the {{ill|lt=Jinzu River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518}} by boat, a white-haired old man appeared and told him that a spring by the pine trees on the riverbank ahead produced {{ill|lt=salt|Q11254|WD=Q11254}} water, and then vanished from sight. Following the old man's words, the party sailed on and found a spring surrounded by a forest. When they boiled down the water, they obtained {{ill|lt=salt|Q11254|WD=Q11254}}. The white-haired old man was the {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Kunitama deity}}, and this was interpreted as a divine oracle to develop this land. A shrine was built to enshrine this deity, and this is the origin of the shrine. The place name "Shio" (salt) also derives from this event. During the Meiji era, the shrine was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine, but "Takuhire" refers to taku cloth (a type of bark cloth), and since this shrine is rather associated with salt, there is also a theory that identifies the Shikinaisha with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Anekurahime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (in Toyama City Kureha-machi) or Gofuku Shrine (in Toyama City Gofuku), which are associated with weaving and clothing. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== {{神社 |名称=Takuhi Reshi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}}{{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}} Shio 690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|display=ti}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) / Former {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建={{ill|lt=672|Q30601|WD=Q30601}} ({{ill|lt=Hakuho|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} 1) |本殿 = |別名=Shio no Miya |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takuhi Reshi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takuhi Reshi Shrine''' (たくひれしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929}} {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Kaminiikawa District|Q11359057|WD=Q11359057}} {{ill|lt=Osawano|Q8081695|WD=Q8081695}}) Shio. Its {{ill|lt=shrine ranking|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|lt=former shrine ranking}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also called '''Shio no Miya''' (しおのみや) after the name of the place where it is enshrined. During the Meiji era, it was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine listed in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki|1=UNKNOWN}} and was renamed to its current name, but there are several other candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 4th month of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Hakuho|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} ({{ill|lt=672|Q30601|WD=Q30601}}), when Hayashi no Sukune Mikaki was traveling upstream on the {{ill|lt=Jinzu River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518}} by boat, a white-haired old man appeared and told him that a spring by the pine trees on the riverbank ahead produced {{ill|lt=salt|Q11254|WD=Q11254}} water, and then vanished from sight. Following the old man's words, the party sailed on and found a spring surrounded by a forest. When they boiled down the water, they obtained {{ill|lt=salt|Q11254|WD=Q11254}}. The white-haired old man was the {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Kunitama deity}}, and this was interpreted as a divine oracle to develop this land. A shrine was built to enshrine this deity, and this is the origin of the shrine. The place name "Shio" (salt) also derives from this event. During the Meiji era, the shrine was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine, but "Takuhire" refers to taku cloth (a type of bark cloth), and since this shrine is rather associated with salt, there is also a theory that identifies the Shikinaisha with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Anekurahime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (in Toyama City Kureha-machi) or Gofuku Shrine (in Toyama City Gofuku), which are associated with weaving and clothing. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

A shrine was built to enshrine this deity, and this is the origin of the shrine. The place name "Shio" (salt) also derives from this event. During the Meiji era, the shrine was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine, but "Takuhire" refers to taku cloth (a type of bark cloth), and since this shrine is rather associated with salt, there is also a theory that identifies the Shikinaisha with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Anekurahime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (in Toyama City Kureha-machi) or Gofuku Shrine (in Toyama City Gofuku), which are associated with weaving and clothing. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is also called '''Shio no Miya''' (しおのみや) after the name of the place where it is enshrined. During the Meiji era, it was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine listed in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki|1=UNKNOWN}} and was renamed to its current name, but there are several other candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}}, {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 4th month of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Hakuho|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=672|Q30601|WD=Q30601|1=672}}), when Hayashi no Sukune Mikaki was traveling upstream on the {{ill|lt=Jinzu River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518|1=Jinzū River}} by boat, a white-haired old man appeared and told him that a spring by the pine trees on the riverbank ahead produced {{ill|lt=salt|Q11254|WD=Q11254|1=Salt}} water, and then vanished from sight. Following the old man's words, the party sailed on and found a spring surrounded by a forest. When they boiled down the water, they obtained {{ill|lt=salt|Q11254|WD=Q11254|1=Salt}}. The white-haired old man was the {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Kunitama deity|1=Kunitama}}, and this was interpreted as a divine oracle to develop this land. A shrine was built to enshrine this deity, and this is the origin of the shrine. The place name "Shio" (salt) also derives from this event. During the Meiji era, the shrine was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine, but "Takuhire" refers to taku cloth (a type of bark cloth), and since this shrine is rather associated with salt, there is also a theory that identifies the Shikinaisha with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Anekurahime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (in Toyama City Kureha-machi) or Gofuku Shrine (in Toyama City Gofuku), which are associated with weaving and clothing. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[File:Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}} Shio 690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|display=ti}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}}
{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}
{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) / Former {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=672|Q30601|WD=Q30601|1=672}} ({{ill|lt=Hakuho|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 1) |本殿 = |別名=Shio no Miya |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takuhi Reshi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takuhi Reshi Shrine''' (たくひれしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Kaminiikawa District|Q11359057|WD=Q11359057|1=Kaminiikawa District, Toyama}} {{ill|lt=Osawano|Q8081695|WD=Q8081695|1=Ōsawano, Toyama}}) Shio. Its {{ill|lt=shrine ranking|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Shio no Miya''' (しおのみや) after the name of the place where it is enshrined. During the Meiji era, it was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine listed in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki|1=UNKNOWN}} and was renamed to its current name, but there are several other candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}}, {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 4th month of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Hakuho|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=672|Q30601|WD=Q30601|1=672}}), when Hayashi no Sukune Mikaki was traveling upstream on the {{ill|lt=Jinzu River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518|1=Jinzū River}} by boat, a white-haired old man appeared and told him that a spring by the pine trees on the riverbank ahead produced {{ill|lt=salt|Q11254|WD=Q11254|1=Salt}} water, and then vanished from sight. Following the old man's words, the party sailed on and found a spring surrounded by a forest. When they boiled down the water, they obtained {{ill|lt=salt|Q11254|WD=Q11254|1=Salt}}. The white-haired old man was the {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Kunitama deity|1=Kunitama}}, and this was interpreted as a divine oracle to develop this land. A shrine was built to enshrine this deity, and this is the origin of the shrine. The place name "Shio" (salt) also derives from this event. During the Meiji era, the shrine was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine, but "Takuhire" refers to taku cloth (a type of bark cloth), and since this shrine is rather associated with salt, there is also a theory that identifies the Shikinaisha with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Anekurahime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (in Toyama City Kureha-machi) or Gofuku Shrine (in Toyama City Gofuku), which are associated with weaving and clothing. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たくひれししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

During the Meiji era, it was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine listed in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki|1=UNKNOWN}} and was renamed to its current name, but there are several other candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}}, {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 4th month of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Hakuho|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=672|Q30601|WD=Q30601|1=672}}), when Hayashi no Sukune Mikaki was traveling upstream on the {{ill|lt=Jinzu River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518|1=Jinzū River}} by boat, a white-haired old man appeared and told him that a spring by the pine trees on the riverbank ahead produced {{ill|lt=salt|Q11254|WD=Q11254|1=Salt}} water, and then vanished from sight. Following the old man's words, the party sailed on and found a spring surrounded by a forest. When they boiled down the water, they obtained {{ill|lt=salt|Q11254|WD=Q11254|1=Salt}}. The white-haired old man was the {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Kunitama deity|1=Kunitama}}, and this was interpreted as a divine oracle to develop this land. A shrine was built to enshrine this deity, and this is the origin of the shrine. The place name "Shio" (salt) also derives from this event. During the Meiji era, the shrine was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine, but "Takuhire" refers to taku cloth (a type of bark cloth), and since this shrine is rather associated with salt, there is also a theory that identifies the Shikinaisha with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Anekurahime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (in Toyama City Kureha-machi) or Gofuku Shrine (in Toyama City Gofuku), which are associated with weaving and clothing. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:越中国の式内社]] [[Category:富山県の旧郷社]]

[[File:Takuhireshi-Jinja (6)t.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}} Shio 690 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|35|46|N|137|11|24|E|display=ti}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}}
{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}
{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) / Former {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=672|Q30601|WD=Q30601|1=672}} ({{ill|lt=Hakuho|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 1) |本殿 = |別名=Shio no Miya |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Toyama|label=Takuhi Reshi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takuhi Reshi Shrine''' (たくひれしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Toyama Prefecture|Q132929|WD=Q132929|1=Toyama Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Toyama City|Q204266|WD=Q204266|1=Toyama (city)}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Kaminiikawa District|Q11359057|WD=Q11359057|1=Kaminiikawa District, Toyama}} {{ill|lt=Osawano|Q8081695|WD=Q8081695|1=Ōsawano, Toyama}}) Shio. Its {{ill|lt=shrine ranking|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=district shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called '''Shio no Miya''' (しおのみや) after the name of the place where it is enshrined. During the Meiji era, it was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine listed in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki|1=UNKNOWN}} and was renamed to its current name, but there are several other candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|1=Hoori}}, {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}, and {{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, in the 4th month of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Hakuho|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} ({{ill|lt=672|Q30601|WD=Q30601|1=672}}), when Hayashi no Sukune Mikaki was traveling upstream on the {{ill|lt=Jinzu River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518|1=Jinzū River}} by boat, a white-haired old man appeared and told him that a spring by the pine trees on the riverbank ahead produced {{ill|lt=salt|Q11254|WD=Q11254|1=Salt}} water, and then vanished from sight. Following the old man's words, the party sailed on and found a spring surrounded by a forest. When they boiled down the water, they obtained {{ill|lt=salt|Q11254|WD=Q11254|1=Salt}}. The white-haired old man was the {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Kunitama deity|1=Kunitama}}, and this was interpreted as a divine oracle to develop this land. A shrine was built to enshrine this deity, and this is the origin of the shrine. The place name "Shio" (salt) also derives from this event. During the Meiji era, the shrine was identified as the Shikinaisha Takuhi Reshi Shrine, but "Takuhire" refers to taku cloth (a type of bark cloth), and since this shrine is rather associated with salt, there is also a theory that identifies the Shikinaisha with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Anekurahime Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (in Toyama City Kureha-machi) or Gofuku Shrine (in Toyama City Gofuku), which are associated with weaving and clothing. === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takuhi Reshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:富山市の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]]
